summary: local-mcp performs install-time download and execution from a Railway-hosted endpoint with system info collection
details: On install, the package fetches and executes content from https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app — a generic Railway PaaS hostname unrelated to the package's stated 'local-mcp' identity — alongside fetches from local-mcp.com / download.local-mcp.com. The install-time scripts (setup.js, download.js, index.js) combine child_process spawning, https/http fetches, filesystem writes, os module enumeration, and process.env access in the canonical install-time dropper shape: download remote payload to disk, execute with elevated process, exfiltrate environment data via POST. setup.js contains multiple POST request paths (lines 61, 343, 800, 878, 904) consistent with telemetry/exfiltration of host data. The mismatch between the package's claimed namespace (local-mcp.com) and the unrelated office-mcp-production.up.railway.app endpoint hardcoded into the install path indicates an attacker-controlled C2, not a publisher-owned distribution CDN. The Railway free-tier hostname is mutable infrastructure with no version pinning or integrity verification on fetched bytes.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
